hypothesis: SUPERSEDED→AUTO-HIGH reduces manual triage and regression tests catch FP rate regressions

method.change_1: SUPERSEDED→AUTO-HIGH: detect SUPERSEDED markers on falsified lessons, auto-classify all uncorrected citers as content_dependent (HIGH priority). Based on L-752 finding that SUPERSEDED chains are 100% content-dependent.
method.change_2: Regression tests (test_correction_propagation.py): 7 tests covering known falsified detection, corrector non-flagging, FP rate <20% bound, SUPERSEDED auto-HIGH, and queue priority.

impact.superseded_auto_high_effect: 3 SUPERSEDED citers auto-classified as HIGH (content_dependent) without keyword-based classification. Eliminates false MEDIUM/LOW for SUPERSEDED chains.
impact.regression_tests_added: 7 tests: 2 detection, 2 corrector-exclusion, 1 FP-rate, 1 auto-HIGH, 1 queue. Catches regressions at N>900.
impact.manual_triage_reduction: SUPERSEDED chains no longer require manual citation-type classification — all auto-HIGH. ~30% of previous HIGH items were SUPERSEDED.

falsification_criteria: If SUPERSEDED auto-HIGH produces false HIGH at >5% rate (non-content-dependent citers auto-classified) over next 10 sessions.
